arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola says Kevin De Bruyne 'saved him from the sack' with Leyton Orient winner
arts.sum: Manchester City needed super-subs Abdukodir Khusanov and Kevin De Bruyne to rescue them after League One Leyton Orient threatened to cause an upset against Pep Guardiola's side

arts.ttl: Real Madrid in the UCL? Right now, Man City struggle to beat Leyton Orient
arts.sum: Real Madrid will hardly be trembling with fear when they watch Manchester City's laboured 2-1 win at Leyton Orient in the FA Cup.

arts.ttl: Rico Lewis reveals half-time message that inspired Man City to comeback win over Leyton Orient in FA Cup
arts.sum: Out of the EFL Cup and sitting 15 points behind Liverpool in the Premier League, the FA Cup looks like City's best hope of serious silverware in 2025. Those hopes were tested at Brisbane Road.

arts.ttl: Nico Gonzalez suffers injury 22 minutes into Man City debut as Pep Guardiola says ‘welcome to England’
arts.sum: The £49m midfielder is a doubt for Tuesday’s Champions League match against Real Madrid after he was forced off against Leyton Orient

arts.ttl: Why Leyton Orient player wasn't sent off vs Man City for Omar Marmoush tackle as rule explained
arts.sum: Manchester City news as Omar Marmoush a serious injury after a bad challenge in the FA Cup win over Leyton Orient

arts.ttl: Lewis: 'I'm happy Khusanov got the goal!'
arts.sum: “It’s gone down as Khusanov’s, which I’m happy for to be fair,” declared Lewis after the game in an interview with City TV. Rico Lewis has expressed his delight after Abdukodir Khusanov was credited with his first goal for City, after the Englishman's strike deflected in off the Uzbek in the 2-1 FA Cup win over Leyton Orient.

arts.ttl: 'A pity' - Pep Guardiola gives Nico Gonzalez injury update after Man City debut
arts.sum: Manchester City new boy Nico Gonzalez was forced off with injury after 22 minutes after losing possession for the first goal

arts.ttl: Man City survive Leyton Orient scare to advance in FA Cup
arts.sum: Kevin De Bruyne struck in the 79th minute to spare the blushes of Manchester City and earn them a hard-fought 2-1 victory in a rip-roaring FA Cup fourth-round tie at Leyton Orient.

arts.ttl: Man City given scare by third-tier Leyton Orient but advances in the FA Cup
arts.sum: But City fought back after the break with goals from substitutes Abdukodir Khusanov and Kevin De Bruyne. Orient went ahead in spectacular fashion when Jamie Donley’s shot from just past the halfway line hit the bar and rebounded off the back of City goalkeeper Stefan Ortega into the back of the net in the 16th minute at Brisbane Road.

arts.ttl: Manchester City get taste of Leyton Orient but De Bruyne seals Cup recovery
arts.athr: Barney Ronay at the Gaughan Group Stadium
arts.sum: It took the full 90 minutes, a tactical rejig, five substitutes and the introduction of two PFA players’ player of the year winners, but Manchester City eventually succeeded in reeling in Leyton Orient. Kevin De Bruyne’s winner completed a 2-1 victory for City in this fourth round FA Cup tie after Orient had led at half through a goalkeeping own goal made by a sublime intervention from Jamie Donley.
